knowledge.title: Inferno A site: pit + balcony crossfire
knowledge.description: CT A: one player pit (close angle on apartments exit), one player site/balcony (long angle from CT spawn). Pit traps apps push, balcony trades. Avoid both on site — apps push frags both with one molly.
knowledge.situation: CT A defense, apartments threat
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pit-balcony is standard CT A setup on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno B site: car + new box anchor
knowledge.description: CT B: one player car (close angle on banana), one player new box (back of site). Car traps banana push, new box trades. The off-angle behind banana smoke is also viable but riskier.
knowledge.situation: CT B defense, banana threat
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: car-newbox crossfire is the dominant Inferno B setup

knowledge.title: Inferno banana: top banana CT with utility
knowledge.description: CT banana player holds top banana with molly + flash. The molly delays T banana push by ~3 seconds; the flash supports a peek for the first frag. Losing top banana control loses ~30% of B round win expectation.
knowledge.situation: CT banana defense
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top banana control predicts B round outcome on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno A execute: smoke CT + smoke library + molly pit
knowledge.description: A execute: smoke CT (cuts rotate from CT spawn), smoke library (cuts the long angle), molly pit (clears the close hold), flash from short for entry. ~72% plant rate on Inferno A with full utility.
knowledge.situation: T-side A execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full-utility A execute is the canonical Inferno T A strat

knowledge.title: Inferno B execute: smoke CT + smoke coffin + molly newbox
knowledge.description: B execute from banana: smoke CT (cuts the rotate), smoke coffin (cuts the off-angle), molly newbox (clears the anchor), flash from banana entry. Plant for banana angle. ~70% plant rate with full B execute.
knowledge.situation: T-side B execute via banana
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full-utility B execute is most reliable Inferno B strat

knowledge.title: Inferno banana molly: deny top banana control
knowledge.description: Throw a molly to top banana before any T banana push. The molly delays the CT hold by ~3 seconds and burns CT utility (they often respond with a flash or smoke). Cheap, high-value setup utility.
knowledge.situation: T banana push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top banana molly is in ~80% of T banana pushes on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno pistol: CT A stack
knowledge.description: Inferno T pistol typically goes B (banana is the easier pistol push) so CTs should stack 3 B with banana molly + close-angle holds. 2 A passive. T A pistol pushes are rare and CT can rotate.
knowledge.situation: Pistol round CT setup
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: T pistol B push rate on Inferno is ~70%

knowledge.title: Inferno force buy: B through banana with molly
knowledge.description: Inferno B force buys (Galil + flash + molly) work because banana is utility-driven, not aim-driven. A forces on Inferno have low success — apartments has too many angles to clear under-buy.
knowledge.situation: T force buy decision on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Inferno B force wins ~40%, A force ~22%

knowledge.title: Inferno save: exit through CT spawn rotates
knowledge.description: Lost-round saves on Inferno: route back through CT spawn or T ramp. Avoid banana saves — CT pushes on lost rounds catch banana savers from above. ~70% rifle retention rate possible with disciplined saves.
knowledge.situation: Inferno save round
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 Inferno save retention rate ~70%

knowledge.title: Inferno apartments duel: pre-aim head level on balcony exit
knowledge.description: Apartments-to-A push: pre-aim head level on the balcony exit angle. The CT will be holding from balcony or pit — both are head-level pre-aims at common spots. Mis-aimed crosshair = automatic loss.
knowledge.situation: T apps to A push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top players hold apps angles with head-level pre-aim >90%

knowledge.title: Inferno banana duel: shoulder peek bait the AWP
knowledge.description: Before banana push, shoulder peek to bait the CT AWP. If you draw the shot, push immediately — the AWPer is in scope-out for ~0.4s. Banana AWP duels are decided by who initiates with bait.
knowledge.situation: Banana AWP duel
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: shoulder peek + commit on no-shot wins ~55% of banana AWP duels

knowledge.title: Inferno close site: spray through smoke is standard
knowledge.description: Inferno sites have so many smokes thrown that wallbang spray accounts for ~15% of site frags. Spray through smokes at chest level on a re-entry — if the CT is repositioning, you'll catch them in the smoke transit.
knowledge.situation: Site retake or entry through smokes
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: smoke wallbang accounts for ~15% of Inferno site frags

knowledge.title: Inferno apps contact: commit to A or fall back to mid
knowledge.description: If you take a trade in apps, commit to A within 5 seconds — the CT has rotated. Slow play after apps contact gives time for CT re-rotates. Commit or fall back, never sit.
knowledge.situation: Apps trade, deciding next move
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: post-apps-contact A commit wins ~58% on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno IGL: banana info is the highest-value read
knowledge.description: On every default round, send a banana player for early info. Banana sound cues confirm CT B count and trigger the A vs B commit. Banana info is the highest-value information point on Inferno.
knowledge.situation: T default information gathering
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 Inferno IGLs base ~60% of commit calls on banana info

knowledge.title: Inferno B fake: 25-second commit
knowledge.description: Inferno B fakes commit utility for 25 seconds (smokes + molly + gunfire) then re-rotate to A through apps. CT will burn rotates. A becomes 4v3 with full utility. 25 seconds is the sweet spot — shorter fakes don't pull rotates.
knowledge.situation: B fake into A execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 25-second B fake to A execute wins ~58% on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno A retake: from arch + library with smoke + flash
knowledge.description: A retake from arch and library with smoke (cuts the lurk) + flash + 3-stack. Avoid pit retakes — pit is the worst angle to push from on Inferno. Arch retakes win ~50% with full utility.
knowledge.situation: CT retake A site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: arch retakes win ~50% on Inferno A

knowledge.title: Inferno B post-plant: hold from coffin or banana lurk
knowledge.description: Post-plant B: lurk back to coffin OR back to banana corner. Coffin holds the CT close angle; banana lurk holds the rotate from A. Choose based on which retake direction the CT is most likely to take.
knowledge.situation: T post-plant B, choosing lurk
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: coffin and banana lurks both outperform site-hold by ~10 points

knowledge.title: Inferno A plant: in front of pit, exposed to balcony
knowledge.description: Default A plant is in front of pit, exposed to balcony. The lurker can pre-fire balcony defuse. Avoid plants behind site — the CT defuses from cover.
knowledge.situation: T A plant location
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: front-of-pit plant has highest post-plant WR on Inferno A

knowledge.title: Inferno anti-strat: counter banana stack with apps split
knowledge.description: If the CT keeps stacking 3 banana, send 4 through apps to A and 1 lurk banana. The banana lurk uses utility to fake a B push while the main team takes the underdefended A site. CTs burning utility on banana waste their retake resources.
knowledge.situation: CT stacking banana with 3 players on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: apps split against banana stack has ~65% A plant rate on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno anti-strat: counter apps push with pit molly
knowledge.description: If the T keeps hitting A through apps, molly the apps exit at the 0:45 mark — this is when most apps pushes time their execute. The molly forces a 7-second delay, giving the CT time to reset crossfire. Follow with a flash to pit for the counter-peek.
knowledge.situation: CT losing A site to apps pushes on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: timed apps exit molly reduces T A success to ~35%

knowledge.title: Inferno entry: banana entry with trade setup
knowledge.description: Entry through banana requires a 2-man push: first player peeks car angle, second trades from sandbags. The entry always checks car first (highest threat), then sweeps to site. Never solo-push banana — the crossfire from car + new box kills solo entries every time.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger on Inferno B push through banana
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 2-man banana push with trade setup succeeds ~55% vs ~25% solo

knowledge.title: Inferno AWP: banana AWP from top banana
knowledge.description: The CT AWP from top banana controls the entire chokepoint. Hold from the left side of the wall for minimal exposure. After one kill, drop back to car or B site — staying top banana after firing is a death sentence against a coordinated push.
knowledge.situation: AWP player holding banana on Inferno CT
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top banana AWP averages 0.7 opening kills per half

knowledge.title: Inferno lurk: second mid lurk during B execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes B through banana, the lurker holds second mid (near archside). CTs rotating from A through arch walk into the lurk. Time the ambush for 6-8 seconds after the B execute smokes land.
knowledge.situation: Lurker role on T-side Inferno during B execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: second mid lurk catches rotating CTs ~40% of the time on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno support: banana utility sequencing
knowledge.description: On Inferno T-side, the support player is responsible for banana utility: molly top banana first (clear the CT hold), then smoke the half-wall angle, then flash for the entry push. This 3-piece sequence is the foundation of every B execute.
knowledge.situation: Support role on T-side Inferno banana push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full utility sequence banana push plant rate is ~70% vs ~40% with incomplete utility

knowledge.title: Inferno clutch: 1v2 B site use coffin angle
knowledge.description: In a 1v2 on Inferno B as T post-plant, hide behind coffin. CTs retaking from CT spawn must check coffin last — giving you time to hear the defuse and peek. If they split from banana and CT, the coffin angle lets you see both approaches at different timings.
knowledge.situation: 1v2 clutch on Inferno B site as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: coffin angle post-plant on Inferno B wins ~28% of 1v2 T clutches

knowledge.title: Inferno: CT mid aggression for info
knowledge.description: Sending one CT aggressively through second mid early-round can catch the T lurker off guard and provide critical info on whether Ts are splitting A or stacking banana. The info player should peek once and fall back, never commit to a fight.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Inferno, need info on T setup
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: early CT mid aggression on Inferno provides useful info ~60% of rounds

knowledge.title: Inferno: T-side apps control for A split
knowledge.description: Take apartments early and hold it passively until the team is ready to commit. Apartments control gives you a flanking path to A — the split from apps + short catches the pit player from two angles. Do not push through apps without the team ready to commit from short.
knowledge.situation: T-side Inferno, planning an A split
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: apps + short A split has ~68% plant rate when both entries are synchronized

knowledge.title: Inferno: B retake utility — smoke banana, molly dark
knowledge.description: CT retaking B must smoke banana (cuts the lurk) and molly dark corner (clears the off-angle). Without these two pieces, the retake faces 3+ angles simultaneously. Save at least 1 smoke and 1 molly for B retakes as the support player.
knowledge.situation: CT retake B site on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: B retakes with smoke+molly succeed ~48% vs ~22% without utility on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno IGL: banana timing calls for B read
knowledge.description: Listen for banana utility at the 0:15 and 0:35 marks. Early banana molly at 0:15 signals a fast B take; utility at 0:35 indicates a slow default with late commit. Adjust your B stack based on which timing you hear — early utility means rotate a third player B immediately.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Inferno, IGL reading banana timing for B defense
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: banana utility timing correctly predicts B execute speed in ~60% of T defaults

knowledge.title: Inferno support: A retake smoke arch + flash for push
knowledge.description: Save one smoke and one flash for A retakes. Smoke the arch entrance to isolate the A lurker from reinforcements, then flash from library for the retake push. This two-piece combo isolates the T post-plant players and raises A retake success by ~12 points.
knowledge.situation: Support player holding utility for A retake on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: arch smoke + library flash A retake succeeds ~52% vs ~35% dry on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno clutch: 1v1 A site — use pit angle post-plant
knowledge.description: In a 1v1 on Inferno A as T post-plant, plant for pit and hold from pit corner. The CT must cross the open site to reach the bomb, and pit gives you the first-shot advantage. If they approach from library, you hear them before they see you.
knowledge.situation: 1v1 clutch on Inferno A site as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pit angle post-plant on Inferno A wins ~58% of 1v1 T clutches

knowledge.title: Inferno AWP: arch angle from site
knowledge.description: Hold the arch exit from A site with the AWP. The arch opening is narrow and the T must fully commit to exit. Position behind the short-side pillar for minimal exposure. After one kill, reposition to balcony — the T team will utility the arch angle on the next attempt.
knowledge.situation: CT AWP holding arch on Inferno A side
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: arch AWP catches T rotators ~35% of the time on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno anti-strat: counter apps push with balcony aggression
knowledge.description: If the T keeps pushing through apps to A, send one player aggressively to balcony to hold the apps exit from above. The elevation advantage gives you the first shot. Fall back to site after one kill — staying balcony after contact exposes you to the second T entry.
knowledge.situation: CT losing A to repeated apps pushes on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: balcony aggression counters apps pushes ~50% of the time on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno entry: second mid push for A split
knowledge.description: Enter through second mid toward arch for an A split. Clear the corner at the second mid entrance, then push toward arch with a flash. The A split from apps + arch catches the pit player from two angles. Never push second mid without the apps team ready to commit simultaneously.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger pushing second mid for A split on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: second mid A split entry succeeds ~50% when synchronized with apps push

knowledge.title: Inferno lurk: arch lurk during B execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes B through banana, lurk at arch side. CTs rotating from A through CT spawn pass the arch sightline. Time the ambush for 7-9 seconds after the B execute smokes — this is when the A players begin their rotate.
knowledge.situation: Lurker on T-side Inferno during B execute, arch position
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: arch lurk catches A-to-B rotators ~38% of the time on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno economy: pistol round T banana with upgraded pistol
knowledge.description: On T pistol, buy P250 + flash and hit banana. The P250 two-taps at banana distances and the flash enables the push past car. Stack 4 banana with utility, leave 1 passive apps for a late A fake. Plant for banana angle and play post-plant.
knowledge.situation: T pistol round strategy on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: P250 banana stack on T pistol wins ~55% on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno positioning: mid control from CT arch side
knowledge.description: Hold mid from the arch-side angle behind the wall. This position covers second mid and the T passage toward A without exposing you to the banana sightline. One player mid with this angle frees the team to stack 2-2 on sites instead of 2-1-2.
knowledge.situation: CT mid control on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: arch-side mid hold provides info on T movement in ~55% of rounds

knowledge.title: Inferno B retake: dark corner molly + CT push
knowledge.description: CT retaking B must molly dark corner before pushing from CT spawn. Dark is the highest-probability post-plant position on B. Follow the molly with a flash and push from CT spawn. Without the dark molly, the post-plant T gets a free kill ~45% of the time.
knowledge.situation: CT retaking B site from CT spawn on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: dark molly + CT push retake succeeds ~45% on Inferno B

knowledge.title: Inferno utility: banana flash chain for entry
knowledge.description: Chain two flashes in banana — the first pops at car to blind the close hold, the second pops at half-wall to blind the deep angle. Space them 1.5 seconds apart. The double flash creates a 3-second blind window where the entry fragger pushes through banana unchallenged.
knowledge.situation: T banana push with flash chain on Inferno
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: double flash banana chain enables entry kills in ~55% of B pushes

knowledge.title: Inferno mid-round: speed control after banana trade
knowledge.description: After winning a banana trade, wait 5 seconds before committing to B site. The 5-second pause lets you hear the CT rotate and decide whether to execute B or fall back. Instant B commits after a banana trade run into stacked retakes ~40% of the time.
knowledge.situation: T-side Inferno, post-banana-trade decision
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 5-second pause after banana trade improves B execute success by ~10 points on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno IGL: second-half T adjustment read
knowledge.description: When switching to T side on Inferno, review the CT defense patterns from the first half. If the enemy played aggressive banana every round, open with a fast A execute to exploit the weak A rotation. If they stacked A with three players, default banana and force the B retake. Commit to the adjustment in round 16 before the CTs settle into a new rhythm.
knowledge.situation: T-side Inferno, second-half opening rounds as IGL
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: IGLs who exploit first-half CT tendencies win ~10% more opening rounds after half-switch on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno IGL: timeout trigger on apartment control collapse
knowledge.description: Call a timeout on Inferno after losing two rounds where the CTs took apartment control early. Inferno A depends on apartment presence — losing it repeatedly means the CT aggression timing is catching your setup. Use the timeout to set a pre-flash at apartments balcony, adjust your mid player to cover the apartment push, and designate a dedicated apartment anchor.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Inferno, losing apartment control repeatedly
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: timeouts after losing map control on Inferno recover the setup in ~55% of cases

knowledge.title: Inferno lurk: alternate route through T apartments to pit
knowledge.description: As the T lurker on Inferno, push through apartments and drop into pit while your team fakes banana. The apartments-to-pit route is rarely watched after the first 20 seconds because CTs rotate toward banana. Time your pit drop with the banana fake utility — the noise covers the drop sound. From pit you have a direct angle on A site retakes.
knowledge.situation: T-side Inferno, lurk during banana fake
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pit lurk flanks produce a kill in ~30% of banana fake rounds on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno lurk: CT banana flank on A retake
knowledge.description: As the CT lurker on Inferno, hold banana during an A site retake. When your team pushes A from arch and library, push banana into B site to cut off the T escape. Time your push to arrive at B 3 seconds after the A retake begins — Ts rotating from A to save will run into your crosshair in the construction area.
knowledge.situation: CT-side Inferno, flanking during A retake
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: banana flanks during A retakes catch rotating Ts ~35% of the time on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno clutch: 1v1 CT defuse on B site from headshot box
knowledge.description: In a 1v1 CT defuse situation on Inferno B, use headshot box (dark) as your anchor position. Tap the defuse to force the T to peek from construction or new box, then hold the angle. The headshot box gives you a tight angle with only your head exposed. If the T holds without peeking, commit to a smoke defuse — throw the smoke on the bomb and defuse through it.
knowledge.situation: 1v1 CT clutch on Inferno B site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: headshot box 1v1 defuse attempts succeed ~42% of the time on Inferno B

knowledge.title: Inferno clutch: 1v3 T bomb timer play on A site
knowledge.description: In a 1v3 T post-plant on Inferno A, plant for pit and retreat to the pit corner. From pit, you have line of sight on the bomb and a head-glitch angle that the CTs must clear before defusing. Use a molotov on the bomb when the timer hits 10 seconds. The three CTs must push one at a time through the narrow balcony or library path — pick them off sequentially rather than taking a multi-angle fight.
knowledge.situation: 1v3 T post-plant clutch on Inferno A site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pit post-plant position with A plant wins ~18% of 1v3 situations on Inferno A

knowledge.title: Inferno aim: top mid to arch duel spot
knowledge.description: Hold the top mid to arch angle from the T spawn corner head-glitch. This position gives you a long-range head-level angle on any CT pushing through arch for mid control. Pre-aim the right edge of the arch doorway — CTs swing wide to clear mid and expose themselves. First-bullet accuracy wins this duel; avoid spraying at this range.
knowledge.situation: T-side Inferno, holding mid against arch push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top mid head-glitch wins the arch duel ~55% of the time at pro level on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno aim: banana wallbang through the wood stack
knowledge.description: The wooden logs at the bottom of banana on Inferno are wallbangable with AK-47 and M4. When you hear footsteps behind the logs, spray through the wood at crouch height. The penetration deals ~40% rifle damage per hit. This is especially effective during anti-eco rounds when CTs force with pistols and no head armor — three to four bullets through the logs secure the kill.
knowledge.situation: T-side Inferno, wallbanging banana logs
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: banana log wallbangs score kills in ~12% of banana contact rounds on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno support: B execute triple-utility combo
knowledge.description: As the support player during an Inferno B execute, throw the CT smoke (blocking CT spawn rotation), the site molotov (clearing dark/headshot box), and the construction flash (blinding the new box anchor). Execute in this order: smoke first to cut off rotations, molotov second to flush the close anchor, flash last to enable the entry. This 7-second combo isolates the B site to one or zero defenders.
knowledge.situation: T-side Inferno, supporting B site execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full CT-dark-construction utility combo raises B execute success to ~60% on Inferno

knowledge.title: Inferno entry: alternate A entry through boiler room
knowledge.description: Instead of the standard apartment push, enter A site through boiler room with a left-hand pop flash. The boiler room path bypasses the apartment crossfire and puts you directly on A site behind the pit player. Flash off the boiler room wall so it pops above the site — this blinds the library and site players. Swing immediately after the flash to catch the A anchor repositioning.
knowledge.situation: T-side Inferno, alternate A entry through boiler
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: boiler room entries score first kill ~45% of the time on Inferno A

knowledge.title: Inferno economy: banana force-buy with SMGs
knowledge.description: On Inferno eco rounds as T, buy three MP9/MAC-10s for a fast banana rush. The narrow banana corridor makes SMG spray effective within 15 meters. Stack all three SMGs behind the corner and rush simultaneously — the close-range burst overwhelms the banana AWPer before they can reposition. Even trading 2-for-1 is profitable since SMGs cost $1050-1250 versus the CT rifles lost.
knowledge.situation: T-side Inferno, eco round banana stack
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: banana SMG rushes on eco rounds average 1.6 kills and break the CT economy ~25% of the time on Inferno
